iteration1.description: Initial targeted skill - identical to original verbose skill with curl commands
iteration1.notes: Model generated 65k tokens of rambling text without making any tool calls. Failed to load skill and failed to use execute_command.

iteration2.description: Rewrote with complete Python scripts using urllib, aggressive content reduction, explicit directive to use execute_command
iteration2.notes: Compare task worked perfectly (3 tool calls). Travel task hit same rambling failure - model variance with tool calling.

iteration3.notes: Both tasks perfect. Confirms iteration 2 skill is effective, previous failure was model variance.

iteration4.notes: Both tasks perfect again. Skill is stable.

changes: Replaced curl shell commands with complete Python scripts using urllib.request and json
Added explicit directive: 'ALWAYS use execute_command to run a Python script' in both description and body
Added anti-pattern warning: 'NEVER just describe what you would do - execute the code'
Reduced skill from 113 lines to ~55 lines (51% reduction)
Removed 'When to Use/When NOT to Use' sections, format codes reference, and generic examples
Added task-specific patterns: multi-city comparison table and travel weather comparison with packing advice
Used concrete Python scripts that can be copy-pasted directly into execute_command
key_insights: devstral-small fails with shell/curl commands - complete Python scripts are much more reliable
The model occasionally fails to make tool calls at all, generating text loops instead (model variance)
Putting actionable instructions in the skill description helps since it appears in the system prompt
Task-aligned code examples dramatically improve reliability over generic reference material
